title: HBR-QUIET-004 foreground exception declaration + operator warning surface
goal: Per spec §6.6.7 (AC-QUIET-FOREGROUND-EXCEPTION) implement the opt-in foreground exception: a WP needing OS-foreground interaction declares `packet.requires_foreground = true` before any run, uses a bounded controlled test window with timeout + auto-dismiss, and surfaces an operator-visible warning (Tauri notification + Diagnostics-panel banner) before launch.
expected_diff_shape: New module src/backend/handshake_core/src/operator_foreground/foreground_exception.rs; controlled-test-window helper; packet validator that enforces requires_foreground declaration before any HBR-QUIET-004 use; Tauri notification + diag-banner emission.
unblock_conditions: MT-014 quiet window foundation; MT-015 focus audit (foreground exception runs trigger focus events that must be flagged in the audit as expected-foreground, not violations).
implementation_notes: Per spec §6.6.7 the default is forbidden; exceptions must be justified and time-bounded. API: `ForegroundException::declare(wp_id: &str, reason: &str, max_duration: Duration) -> ForegroundExceptionHandle` writes a row to a `foreground_exception_log` (per-WP JSONL under .GOV/runtime/foreground_log/<wp_id>.jsonl) and triggers (a) a Tauri notification via `tauri-plugin-notification` (add to Cargo.toml if not present), (b) a Diagnostics-panel banner (Diag panel proper lands in cluster X; this MT emits a typed `DIAG_BANNER_REQUEST` event the panel subscribes to). The handle's `.bounded_window(label, url) -> ControlledWindow` returns a SHOWN, FOCUSED window that lives for at most max_duration and auto-dismisses via tokio::time::timeout. On dismiss, the handle records an end-row. Packet validator: when MT-002 hbr-matrix-hydrate runs against a packet, if any HBR-QUIET-004 row is emitted Applicable, the validator REQUIRES `packet.requires_foreground == true` else emits a violation (this MT extends MT-002 hydrator's checks with this rule). Tests: declare an exception, build a controlled window, assert notification fires + banner event emitted + focus audit (from MT-015) tags the foreground transitions with `expected_foreground=true`; assert auto-dismiss closes the window at deadline; assert packet without requires_foreground=true cannot use ForegroundException::declare (returns Err with explicit reason).
risk_if_missed: Without an explicit foreground exception API, packets that legitimately need foreground (rare) either silently violate HBR-QUIET-001/002 or work around the QuietWindowBuilder ban.
validator_focus: ForegroundException::declare REJECTS calls when packet.requires_foreground=false; auto-dismiss is enforced even if the controlled window doesn't close itself; focus-audit (MT-015) entries are tagged so the harness assertion doesn't false-positive on legitimate foreground use.

minimum_controls: ForegroundException::declare returns Err when packet.requires_foreground is missing or false.
auto-dismiss timeout is enforced via tokio::time::timeout (not best-effort).
Focus audit tags expected_foreground=true on declared exception windows to prevent test false-positives.
